title: Triggers: System
text: Triggers: System System triggers are script modules that are synchronously executed in response to system events that are initiated by commands. System trigger names are predetermined and case-insensitive. For more information, see System Triggers . Activated by: Command or system event Execution: Synchronous Declared by: trigger TriggerName Parameters allowed: No Implementation: ProcScript Related Links trigger...end Declare a trigger. Script Modules A script module is a named collection of ProcScript belonging to a development object. Script modules are analogous to events and methods in other programming languages. Trigger Activation Triggers can be activated by events originating from user actions, ProcScript statements, and external events (such as asynchronous interrupts). Triggers: Interactive Interactive triggers are script modules that are executed in response to events associated with a specific object in a user interface component. They can be fired as a result of user actions, such as clicking a widget or changing its value. Alternatively, some triggers can be invoked by their corresponding macro or the callfieldtrigger ProcScript command. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
